explanation: Glycine soja (soybean) oil is a plant oil rich in linoleic acid, oleic acid, and vitamin E that supports skin barrier repair and provides emollient conditioning. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with soy allergies should note the botanical source; it has moderate comedogenic potential at high concentrations.

Glycine soja (soybean) oil is a plant oil rich in linoleic and oleic fatty acids with emollient and barrier-supporting properties. It is generally well-tolerated by most skin types, though those with soy allergies should note the source; it has moderate comedogenic potential at high concentrations.

Glycine soja (soybean) seed extract provides isoflavones including genistein and daidzein, along with phospholipids and antioxidants with free-radical-scavenging and skin-brightening properties. It is generally well-tolerated, though individuals with soy allergies should approach with caution.

Glycol distearate is an ethylene glycol diester of stearic acid used as a pearlizing agent and opacifier in shampoos, cleansers, and body wash formulas, creating a characteristic silky, pearl-like appearance. It is well-tolerated and non-irritating at typical use concentrations.

Glycolic acid is the gold-standard AHA exfoliant with the smallest molecular size in its class, enabling deep penetration to accelerate cell turnover, resurface skin texture, reduce hyperpigmentation, and stimulate collagen synthesis. At effective concentrations (5–20%) it commonly causes stinging, tingling, and temporary redness — particularly on reactive or barrier-compromised skin — and significantly increases UV sensitivity, requiring strict daily sun protection.

Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice) root extract is rich in glabridin and glycyrrhizic acid with potent anti-inflammatory, tyrosinase-inhibiting, and brightening properties. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and used in formulas targeting post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation, rosacea, and reactive skin for its ability to calm inflammation and reduce melanin production without the irritation of hydroquinone.

Glycyrrhiza uralensis (Chinese licorice) root extract provides the same glabridin and glycyrrhizic acid antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actives as standard licorice root extract, with similar tyrosinase-inhibiting and brightening properties. It is well-tolerated and used in formulas targeting hyperpigmentation and reactive or rosacea-prone skin.

Grape seed extract (Vitis vinifera) is one of the richest plant sources of oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) and resveratrol, providing exceptionally potent antioxidant protection against free-radical damage and UV-induced oxidative stress. It is well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and widely used in anti-aging and protective formulas for its broad-spectrum antioxidant activity.

Green 5 (CI 61570) is a synthetic anthraquinone dye used to impart green tones to cosmetics and personal care products. It is considered well-tolerated in topical applications at typical cosmetic use concentrations.

Green tea (Camellia sinensis) is one of the most studied botanical antioxidants, rich in EGCG and catechins with potent free-radical-scavenging, anti-inflammatory, and UV-protective properties. It is exceptionally well-tolerated by all skin types and is a reliable antioxidant ingredient in protective and anti-aging formulas.

Green tea extract (Camellia sinensis) concentrates the plant's catechin antioxidants including EGCG with potent free-radical-scavenging, anti-inflammatory, and UV-sensitization-protective properties. It is exceptionally well-tolerated by all skin types and is a reliable antioxidant backbone in protective and anti-aging formulas.

Guar hydroxypropyltrimonium chloride is a cationic guar gum derivative used as a conditioning and film-forming agent in hair care and skin care formulas, improving slip, detangling, and surface feel. It is well-tolerated at typical use concentrations and is used in gentle, conditioning cleansers and leave-on formulas.

Haematococcus pluvialis oil is extracted from the astaxanthin-rich microalgae Haematococcus pluvialis and delivers one of the most potent carotenoid antioxidants available — astaxanthin — directly alongside the oil's emollient fatty acids. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and anti-aging formulas for its exceptional free-radical-scavenging and skin-conditioning activity.

Hamamelis virginiana (witch hazel) extract is a botanical astringent rich in tannins, polyphenols, and gallic acid with mild anti-inflammatory and pore-tightening properties. The alcohol-free, steam-distilled form is generally well-tolerated by oily and combination skin; products using witch hazel in a high-alcohol base can be drying and sensitizing on reactive or dry skin.

Hamamelis virginiana (witch hazel) leaf extract provides tannins and polyphenol antioxidants from the witch hazel leaf with mild astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The alcohol-free form is generally well-tolerated on oily and combination skin; high-alcohol extractions can be drying on reactive or dry skin types.

HDI/trimethylol hexyllactone crosspolymer is a crosslinked polyurethane film-former used in cosmetics as a soft-focus texture agent and silicone alternative, creating a smooth, pore-minimizing finish on the skin surface. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, used in primers and lightweight moisturizers for its optical blurring effect.

Helianthus annuus (sunflower) seed oil — the asterisk indicates organic certification — is a lightweight plant oil with high linoleic acid content (~65%) that supports barrier repair and is exceptionally well-tolerated by sensitive and reactive skin. It absorbs readily, has very low comedogenic potential, and is one of the most evidence-supported plant oils for restoring the linoleic acid-deficient barrier seen in atopic and acne-prone skin.

Helianthus annuus (sunflower) seed oil is a lightweight plant oil with high linoleic acid content (~65%) that supports skin barrier repair and is exceptionally well-tolerated by sensitive, reactive, and acne-prone skin. It absorbs readily, has very low comedogenic potential, and is among the most evidence-supported plant oils for restoring barrier function in linoleic acid-deficient skin conditions.

Helianthus annuus (sunflower) seed oil is a lightweight plant oil with high linoleic acid content (~65%) that supports barrier repair and is exceptionally well-tolerated by sensitive and reactive skin. Its low comedogenic rating and evidence base for restoring linoleic acid-deficient barriers makes it suitable across skin types including oily and acne-prone.

Helianthus annuus (sunflower) seed wax is a natural plant wax derived from sunflower seeds, used as a vegan structuring agent and thickener in balms, sticks, and anhydrous formulas. It is considered well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, providing a firm, smooth texture as a natural alternative to beeswax.

Hesperidin is a flavanone glycoside found abundantly in citrus peel with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and mild vascular-strengthening properties, used in skincare to reduce redness, support microcirculation, and protect against oxidative stress. It is well-tolerated and non-irritating, and is used in brightening and calming formulas for reactive and redness-prone skin.

Hexapeptide-11 is a synthetic yeast-derived peptide that supports fibroblast activity and helps improve skin firmness and elasticity by stimulating extracellular matrix components. It is well-tolerated, non-irritating, and used in anti-aging formulas for its firming and skin-renewal properties.

Hexapeptide-9 is a synthetic peptide designed to stimulate collagen IV synthesis in the dermal-epidermal junction, helping to reinforce the structural connection between the dermis and epidermis that weakens with age. It is well-tolerated and non-irritating, used in firming and anti-aging formulas.

Hexyl cinnamal is a synthetic jasmine-like fragrance compound classified as one of 26 known fragrance allergens required to be disclosed by name on EU cosmetic labels. It can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin, particularly with repeated leave-on exposure.

Hexylene glycol is a short-chain diol used in skincare as a solvent, coupling agent for fragrance, and mild co-humectant that helps ingredients dissolve and disperse evenly in a formula. It is generally well-tolerated at typical cosmetic concentrations, though at high concentrations it can be mildly irritating to reactive skin.

Hibiscus esculentus (okra) fruit extract is rich in mucilaginous polysaccharides that form a hydrating, film-like layer on the skin surface to retain moisture and improve skin smoothness. It is well-tolerated and used in hydrating and smoothing formulas for its high-molecular-weight polysaccharide content.

Hibiscus sabdariffa (roselle/hibiscus) flower extract is rich in anthocyanins, organic acids including citric and malic acid, and polyphenol antioxidants with brightening, mild exfoliating, and free-radical-scavenging properties. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and antioxidant formulas for its dual exfoliating acid and polyphenol antioxidant content.

Hippophae rhamnoides (sea buckthorn) fruit extract is an exceptionally nutrient-dense botanical antioxidant containing one of the highest vitamin C concentrations in any fruit, along with carotenoids, flavonoids, and tocopherols. It is well-tolerated and used in brightening and protective formulas for its exceptional antioxidant density.

Hippophae rhamnoides (sea buckthorn) oil is one of the most nutrient-dense plant oils, extremely rich in carotenoids (giving it a vivid orange-red color), omega-7 palmitoleic acid, vitamins C and E, and tocotrienols. It is well-tolerated and particularly valued for dry, mature, and compromised skin, though its intense pigmentation means it is typically used at low concentrations to avoid staining the skin orange.

Homosalate is a chemical UVB sunscreen filter that absorbs UV radiation in the 295–315 nm range and is widely used to enhance the SPF of sunscreen formulas. It has raised regulatory concerns about potential endocrine-disrupting activity at systemic concentrations — the EU has restricted its maximum concentration, and the FDA is reviewing its safety status — making it one of the more scrutinized chemical filters for reactive and hormone-sensitive individuals.
